source.title: Functional Imbalance of Anterolateral Entorhinal Cortex and Hippocampal Dentate/CA3 Underlies Age-Related Object Pattern Separation Deficits

confidence_rationale: Direct human fMRI evidence linking DG/CA3 functional state to behavioural mnemonic discrimination performance. Closest paper in the pull to operationalising 'interference' as a behavioural readout. Confidence moderate because the link to V_s invalidation is via the inference 'mnemonic discrimination = local schema separation = inputs to V_s accumulator' -- defensible but layered.
failure_signatures: alEC hypoactivity + DG/CA3 hyperactivity in aging produces mnemonic discrimination deficits -- a candidate biological signature of failed schema-interference detection. If V_s reads from this substrate, aging populations should show V_s-invalidation failure even when their DA/LHb broadcast triggers are intact, consistent with age-related cognitive rigidity not driven by reward-circuit dysfunction.

mapping.source_claim_statement: High-resolution fMRI in cognitively normal older adults shows alEC hypoactivity together with DG/CA3 hyperactivity, and this functional imbalance correlates with object mnemonic discrimination deficits.
mapping.ree_translation: Mnemonic discrimination is the behavioural proxy for the same operation V_s tracks at a regional-schema level: the ability to register that the current input is meaningfully different from the previously anchored representation. Reagh's alEC-DG/CA3 imbalance is a candidate substrate for V_s-input failure: the local interference signal is broken before it ever reaches the accumulator. The paper supports the broader claim that an entorhinal-hippocampal circuit specifically generates the kind of signal MECH-284 needs.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The behavioural test is object discrimination not schema invalidation. Whether the same circuit operates at the regional-schema scale that V_s requires is an extrapolation. The mapping is at the level of 'this circuit detects subtle differences and is the right anatomical neighbourhood for V_s input', not at the level of measured V_s.
mapping.source_context: Human ultra-high-resolution fMRI in younger and older asymptomatic adults during an object mnemonic discrimination task; n moderate.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron human fMRI study, well executed but small sample. Mapping moderate because behavioural proxy is one step removed from V_s. Transfer low because the species and circuit are right.
